bullets.text: Iran and Israel have reportedly halted strikes following their first exchange of fire since a truce, with Iran launching missiles at Israel after a strike in Lebanon, and Israel conducting air strikes in Iran.
At least 35 people have died after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines, triggering small tsunami waves across the region including in Indonesia and Japan.

Malawians are being repatriated from South Africa due to xenophobia concerns, as Malawi joins other African nations in transporting citizens out of the country following reports of violence against migrants.
A cybersecurity company with reported financial 'red flags' secured a $15 million state contract during the final days of the Miles government.
Plague locust numbers in north-west Victoria have reached their highest levels in 15 years, posing a significant threat to farmers.
Patients in Tasmania are facing years-long waits for elective surgery, as delays and cancellations accumulate within the health system.
Wall Street experienced a rebound, with chip stocks like Intel and Micron seeing significant gains, signaling renewed investor enthusiasm for AI-linked shares.
Amazon made history by selling C$14 billion ($10 billion) in Canadian dollar high-grade bonds, marking the largest corporate debt offering ever in that currency.
China is currently experiencing a paradox where innovation thrives alongside economic challenges, leading to uncertainty about which trend will ultimately prevail.
